summary: Add the verified, bounded loading boundary for separately fetched OpenSCAD JavaScript and WebAssembly artifacts without bundling or statically importing engine bytes.
specSections: FR-4.7 separately fetched and cached web engine

decisions.topic: Verified separate OpenSCAD WASM execution boundary
decisions.decision: Fetch the exact versioned JavaScript and WASM paths as separate assets, enforce their pinned decoded byte lengths and SHA-256 values before any module execution, import verified JavaScript through a revocable Blob URL, pass a defensive WASM copy, and constrain any generated-module locateFile request to a second revocable Blob made from the already verified WASM bytes.
decisions.rationale: The GPL engine artifacts must remain outside the ScadMill application bundle, while executable JavaScript and WASM still require a complete integrity boundary. Exact decoded lengths bound streamed memory before hashing, an AbortSignal lets the worker lifecycle stop stalled transfers, both digests settle before import, and verified Blob URLs prevent a fallback from reopening an unverified network path.

testEvidence.criterion: Only the exact pinned artifact pair can execute, downloads are decoded-length bounded and abortable, and every executable Blob URL is lifecycle bounded
testEvidence.test: Exercise unique ENGINE_VERSION pin binding, HTTP and decoded-length failures, streamed overflow cancellation, truncation, AbortSignal propagation, both hash mismatches, digest failure, deferred dual verification, exact SHA-256 selection, defensive WASM ownership, verified locateFile fallback, module/runtime failure cleanup, progress-observer isolation, and unsupported URLs.

red.command: Independent loader audit; pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-web/openscad-wasm-loader.test.ts
red.outcome: RED: the independent review found 0/0/3/0/0 in the repaired loader; the strengthened focused suite then failed 2 of 18 tests, proving compressed transfer lengths were rejected and an inherited test manifest could displace the production trust anchor. An earlier exact-tree run also exposed and removed a 26-second large-fixture timeout by retaining production-pin assertions while moving behavioral tests to a trusted small-artifact seam.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-web/openscad-wasm-loader.test.ts; pnpm.cmd exec biome check src/platform-web/openscad-wasm-loader.ts tests/platform-web/openscad-wasm-loader.test.ts; pnpm.cmd typecheck
green.outcome: GREEN: 1 focused file and all 18 tests passed in 559 ms; focused Biome checked both files with zero diagnostics; TypeScript exited 0.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
